tree.setup: You're at a street market looking at a scarf.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个围巾很好看！五十块。
turns.text_pinyin: zhè ge wéijīn hěn hǎokàn! wǔshí kuài.
turns.text_en: This scarf is very pretty! Fifty yuan.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Try to negotiate a lower price.

options.text_pinyin: tài guì le. sānshí kuài kěyǐ ma?
options.feedback: Classic haggling — state it's too expensive, then offer.
Good — asking for cheaper. Even better with a specific number.
That just walks away. Try negotiating first!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: sānshí tài shǎo le. sìshí kuài ba.
turns.text_en: Thirty is too little. How about forty?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Close the deal or counter again.

options.feedback: Deal made. 好吧 shows you're accepting gracefully.
Nice counter. 行不行 is a natural way to propose.
You can say this, but offering a number moves things forward.

tree.cultural_note: Haggling is expected at Chinese street markets. Start at about 50-60% of the asking price. 太贵了 (tài guì le) is your best friend. Fixed-price shops don't haggle.
